payload:
KANBAN BOARD SPECIFICATION

## Components
1. Board — root container holding all columns
2. Column — represents one swimlane (Todo, Doing, Done)
3. Card — represents one task inside a column
4. AddCardForm — per-column input+button to create a card

## Data Model (stored as JSON in localStorage key 'kanban')
{
  "columns": [
    { "id": "todo",  "title": "Todo" },
    { "id": "doing", "title": "Doing" },
    { "id": "done",  "title": "Done" }
  ],
  "cards": [
    { "id": "<uuid>", "title": "<string>", "columnId": "<column.id>" }
  ]
}

## Interface Contract (frontend-only, no server)
- On load: read 'kanban' from localStorage; if absent, initialise with three empty columns.
- On every state mutation: serialise full state back to localStorage.
- addCard(columnId, title): creates card with generated id, appends to cards array.
- deleteCard(cardId): removes card from cards array.
- moveCard(cardId, targetColumnId): updates card.columnId.
- Drag-and-drop via native HTML5 draggable API: dragstart stores cardId, drop calls moveCard.
- Each card also shows left/right arrow buttons as fallback move controls.

## User-Facing Behaviours
1. Three visible columns side-by-side.
2. Each column has a text input + 'Add' button at the bottom; submitting adds a card to that column.
3. Cards display their title and a '✕' delete button.
4. Cards are draggable onto any column header/body to move them.
5. Left/right arrow buttons on each card move it one column in that direction (disabled at boundaries).
6. State survives page reload.

## Style Constraints
- Inline CSS only, no external stylesheets.
- Clean neutral palette; columns visually distinct boxes.
- No frameworks, no imports, single index.html file.

{"approved": true, "working": ["Valid HTML5 document starting with <!DOCTYPE html>", "Three Kanban columns present: Todo, Doing, Done", "Add card form (input + button) present in each column with Enter-key support", "Cards moveable via left/right arrow buttons and native HTML5 drag-and-drop", "Delete button (✕) on each card", "State persisted to localStorage under key 'kanban' on every mutation", "Frontend calls window.kanbanAPI (the backend layer) for all operations — layers are wired", "No undefined function references; all IDs and selectors are consistent", "Drag-over visual highlight on columns", "Card count badge in column headers", "Empty column hint text", "Boundary conditions handled: left button disabled on first column, right button disabled on last column", "State loaded on startup with fallback to default if absent or corrupted"], "failing": [], "must_redo": [], "feedback_for_iteration": "No issues found. The artifact is fully functional and meets all specified requirements."}
